prs.title: fix(byok): prevent duplicate provider overwrite
prs.body: Closes #1228 ## Summary Prevent silent overwriting of existing BYOK providers when creating or renaming providers with duplicate names. Previously, the create endpoint used `upsert` keyed on `(workspaceId, provider)`. Creating a provider with an existing name would unexpectedly update the existing provider instead of creating a new one. This change introduces explicit duplicate-name validation and returns `409 Conflict` when a provider with the same name already exists in the workspace. ## Changes * Replace `modelProvider.upsert()` with duplicate lookup + `create()`. * Return `409 Conflict` when creating a provider with an existing name. * Add duplicate-name validation to the PATCH endpoint when renaming providers. * Preserve existing update behavior for the current provider. * Prevent silent overwrites from both create and rename flows. ## Validation * Verified duplicate provider creation returns `409 Conflict`. * Verified duplicate provider rename returns `409 Conflict`. * Verified existing providers are no longer overwritten. * Verified frontend receives the server error message through `fetchNextApi`. --- ## Summary by cubic Prevent silent overwrites of BYOK providers by rejecting duplicate names on create and rename, including race conditions. Returns 409 Conflict instead of updating the existing provider. Closes #1228. - **Bug Fixes** - Replaced `modelProvider.upsert()` with pre-check + `create()` in POST; catch Prisma `P2002` to handle races. - Added duplicate-name validation in PATCH (excludes current provider ID) and `P2002` handling on update. - Preserved normal updates when the name is unchanged. - Added API tests for POST and PATCH covering 409 conflicts, race errors, and success paths. Written for commit 2e21534cb8e2676cfe6c35da26111274fdd62788. Summary will update on new commits.

prs.title: feat(billing): make Slack integration available on all plans
prs.body: Closes #1243 ## Summary Slack integration was previously restricted to Pro and Enterprise plans through the `slack-integration` entitlement. This change makes Slack integration available on all plans while preserving the existing entitlement-based gating architecture. ## Changes * Widen `slack-integration` entitlement to include Free, Starter, Pro, and Enterprise plans. * Remove Slack as a Pro-exclusive marketing feature. * Remove Slack-exclusive wording from Enterprise plan features. * Update Slack install route tests to reflect Starter entitlement access. * Update Slack notification tests to reflect Free and Starter entitlement access. ## Validation * Updated affected unit tests. * Verified entitlement logic remains centralized in `ENTITLEMENT_CONFIG`. * Preserved upgrade redirect test coverage by mocking a non-entitled state. --- ## Summary by cubic Makes Slack integration available on Free, Starter, Pro, and Enterprise by widening the `slack-integration` entitlement. Updates plan copy and tests, removes the `SlackConnectButton` upgrade gate, adds a UI test for the connect flow (no billingPlan -> no upgrade prompt), and preserves defense-in-depth checks (missing plan -> `free`). Fulfills #1243. Written for commit 056586a27fb19b14e92b6656072808922b7961cf. Summary will update on new commits.

prs.title: fix(vercel-ai): preserve user request during synthesis
prs.body: Closes #1207 ## Summary The Vercel AI streaming example was not completing the full user request. The original query asks the agent to both: 1. Research common reliability problems in modern AI inference platforms. 2. Recommend where to invest first. However, the synthesis step only received the generated analyses and was instructed to create a 2-paragraph brief. Since the original request was not passed into the synthesis prompt, the model never received the recommendation requirement and could return a summary without any prioritization. This change passes the original user request into the synthesis step and explicitly instructs the model to provide a ranked recommendation. ## Changes * Pass the original `query` into the synthesis prompt. * Update synthesis instructions to: * Generate a 2-paragraph brief. * Recommend where to invest first. * Provide a clear ranking. ## Validation * Verified that the synthesis phase previously received only the generated analyses. * Confirmed that the recommendation requirement was not propagated to the final synthesis prompt. * Updated the synthesis prompt to preserve the original user intent. * Attempted local execution of `demo:streaming`. * Runtime validation was blocked by OpenAI API quota exhaustion (`insufficient_quota`) and a missing `TRACEROOT_API_KEY`. --- ## Summary by cubic Preserves the user's request in the `vercel-ai` streaming example so the synthesis step returns both the 2-paragraph brief and a ranked recommendation, not just a summary. - **Bug Fixes** - Pass the original `query` into the synthesis prompt to preserve intent. - Update synthesis instructions to write a 2-paragraph brief and then provide a ranked recommendation. Written for commit 14a7d07903041a21a864639dfe66b02bbb68f0d4. Summary will update on new commits.

prs.title: docs: add Japanese README (README.ja.md)
prs.body:
Closes #891

## Summary
Added Japanese translation of README.md as README.ja.md to improve 
accessibility for Japanese-speaking developers.

- Added `README.ja.md` mirroring the structure of the current English `README.md`
- Added Japanese language badge to the language-switcher row
- All images, badges, code samples, and external links kept unchanged — only prose is translated

## Type of Change
- [x] docs - Documentation only changes

## Details
This mirrors the Simplified Chinese README added in #890 and the Korean README in #893.

## Screenshots / Recordings (if applicable)
N/A

## Checklist
- [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] I have added/updated tests where applicable
- [ ] I have added screenshots or recordings for UI changes (if applicable)
- [ ] I have updated documentation where necessary

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Adds a Japanese translation of the README (`README.ja.md`) to improve accessibility for Japanese-speaking developers. Also adds a Japanese badge to the README language switcher; content mirrors the English README with images, badges, code samples, and links unchanged.

<sup>Written for commit ce198e5d332f7955b3009175752cb156ee2ffde0. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

prs.title: Mejorar los mensajes de error del middleware de inquilinos
prs.body:
Descripción (en español)

Los mensajes de error del middleware de inquilinos se han actualizado para que sean más comprensibles y estén en español.

🔹 Todos los mensajes ahora están traducidos al español.
🔹 Se mejoró la claridad de los mensajes para que sean más útiles al usuario final.
🔹 Se añadieron códigos de error únicos (por ejemplo: TENANT_NO_ENCONTRADO, TENANT_INACTIVO).
🔹 Los códigos de error se documentaron dentro del archivo backend/src/middleware/tenant.middleware.js.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

## Notas de la versión

* **Correcciones de errores**
  * Mejorada la validación de inquilinos con verificación de estado activo.
  * Mensajes de error más descriptivos con códigos específicos (TENANT_INACTIVE, TENANT_NOT_FOUND, TENANT_IDENTIFICATION_FAILED).
  * Respuestas de error enriquecidas incluyen sugerencias y detalles de identificación en español.

* **Mejoras**
  * Control de flujo optimizado para gestión de inquilinos inactivos.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Add Zod validation across backend
prs.body:
- Added candidate, vote, and event schemas using Zod  
- Created reusable validateSchema middleware with safeParse and detailed error handling  
- Integrated validation into POST/PUT routes for candidates, votes, and events  
- Added partial schemas for update routes to allow flexible PATCH-like updates  
- Ensured datetime fields follow RFC 3339 and candidate IDs use cuid format  
- Improved type safety, error clarity, and data integrity across backend  
- Verified no lint errors or merge conflicts after integration


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Mejoras**
  * Validación automática de entrada para candidatos, eventos y votos antes de procesarlos.
  * Respuestas de error claras y estructuradas con mensajes y códigos que indican campos problemáticos.
  * Validaciones específicas: límites de longitud, formatos (URL, email, fechas) y formatos de identificador.
  * Comprobación de coherencia de fechas en eventos (inicio anterior a fin).
  * Soporte para actualizaciones parciales en recursos.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

issues.title: Theme Changer Icon on Top for Dark and Light Mode
issues.body: Add a theme switcher icon at the top of the interface that allows users to toggle between dark and light modes seamlessly. The icon should dynamically update based on the current theme

issues.title: Add Currency Generation Feature to the Project
issues.body:
Hi 👋, @Nikhil-2002 
I would like to contribute by adding a currency generation feature to the project.

💡 Proposed Feature:

Implement a system that generates in-game currency (e.g., coins, credits, or points).

Currency can be earned based on specific actions (e.g., completing a level, winning a round, etc.).

Optionally, display the user’s total currency on the UI (like a counter or wallet icon).

Ensure it integrates smoothly with the existing project logic and UI design.

⚙️ Technical Implementation (Planned):

Use a state variable (e.g., score, coins, or balance) to track currency.

Add functions to increase/decrease currency based on events.

Store the value locally (using localStorage or backend API, depending on project setup).

Please:

Label this issue as hacktoberfest2025 🎃

Assign it to me so I can start implementing this feature.

Thank you! 🙌

issues.title: Make Landing Page More Interactive with Animations and Improved UX
issues.body:
Hi 👋, @bhavy1204 
The current landing page looks nice, but it can be made more interactive and engaging for users.

I would like to enhance the UI/UX by adding:

Smooth hover effects on buttons and cards

Subtle animations or transitions for key sections

A scroll reveal effect or fade-in animations

A more dynamic layout for better user engagement

These improvements will make the website feel more modern and user-friendly.

Please:

Label this issue as hacktoberfest2025 🎃

Assign it to me so I can start working on it.

Thank you! 🙌

issues.title: Add game instructions section to the landing page
issues.body:
Hi 👋,
Currently, the landing page doesn’t include any instructions or rules for how to play the game. This can make it confusing for new users who visit the site for the first time.

I’d like to add a short “How to Play” or “Game Instructions” section directly on the landing page.
It will include:

Basic rules of the game

How to start or play (controls, objectives, etc.)

Any special notes or tips for players

Please:

Label this issue as hacktoberfest2025 🎃

Assign it to me so I can start working on it.

Thank you! 🙌
